title: Webhook Delivery via Oban

story.i_want_to: have pending webhook events delivered to subscriber URLs via an Oban background worker with exponential backoff retry
story.so_that: webhook delivery is reliable, non-blocking, and automatically retries transient failures without impacting the main request path

rationale: Webhook delivery must not block the API request that triggered the event. An Oban worker processes pending webhook_events asynchronously, making HTTP POST requests to the subscriber URL. Exponential backoff (1m, 5m, 25m, 2h, 10h) ensures transient failures are retried without overwhelming a down endpoint, while a max of 6 attempts prevents indefinite retries against permanently broken URLs.

acceptance_criteria.description: An Oban worker (Loopctl.Workers.WebhookDeliveryWorker) processes webhook_event records with status='pending'. The worker is enqueued after the transaction that creates the webhook_event commits.
The worker makes an HTTP POST request to the webhook URL with the event payload as the JSON body. Content-Type is application/json.
On successful delivery (HTTP 2xx response), the webhook_event status is updated to 'delivered', delivered_at is set, and the webhook's consecutive_failures is reset to 0.

On failed delivery (non-2xx response, timeout, or connection error), the webhook_event attempts counter is incremented, last_attempt_at is updated, and error is set to a descriptive message (HTTP status code + body snippet, or connection error description).
Retry schedule uses exponential backoff: attempt 1 at +1m, attempt 2 at +5m, attempt 3 at +25m, attempt 4 at +2h, attempt 5 at +10h. Maximum 6 total attempts.
After all retry attempts are exhausted, the webhook_event status is set to 'exhausted'. The webhook's consecutive_failures is incremented.
The HTTP request has a timeout of 10 seconds. Requests exceeding the timeout are treated as failures.
The Oban worker runs in a dedicated 'webhooks' queue with configurable concurrency (default 10).

The worker does not process events for inactive webhooks (active=false). If the webhook was deactivated between event creation and delivery, the event is marked as 'failed' with error='webhook_deactivated'.
Webhook delivery payload includes a unique delivery ID, event type, timestamp, and the event-specific data. Example: {id: uuid, event: 'story.status_changed', timestamp: ISO8601, data: {...}}.

test_cases.name: successful delivery updates event status
preconditions: fixture(:webhook_event, %{webhook_id: webhook.id, tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :pending, payload: %{event: 'story.status_changed'}})
HTTP mock returns 200 OK for POST to https://example.com/hooks
steps: Execute WebhookDeliveryWorker.perform/1 with the webhook_event job
expected_results: HTTP POST was made to https://example.com/hooks with JSON payload

test_cases.name: failed delivery increments attempts and retries
preconditions: fixture(:webhook_event, %{webhook_id: webhook.id, tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :pending, attempts: 0})
HTTP mock returns 500 Internal Server Error
steps: Execute WebhookDeliveryWorker.perform/1 with the webhook_event job
expected_results: webhook_event error contains '500' or server error description
webhook_event status is still 'pending' (not exhausted yet)
Worker returns {:snooze, 60} to schedule retry in 1 minute

test_cases.name: exhausted after max attempts
preconditions: fixture(:webhook, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, url: 'https://example.com/hooks', active: true, consecutive_failures: 5})
fixture(:webhook_event, %{webhook_id: webhook.id, tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :pending, attempts: 5})
HTTP mock returns 500 Internal Server Error
steps: Execute WebhookDeliveryWorker.perform/1 with the webhook_event job (6th attempt)

test_cases.name: timeout treated as failure
preconditions: HTTP mock simulates timeout (no response within 10 seconds)
expected_results: webhook_event error contains 'timeout' or similar description
Treated same as a failed delivery with retry scheduling

test_cases.name: exponential backoff schedule is correct
steps: Call WebhookDeliveryWorker.backoff_seconds/1 with attempt numbers 1 through 5

test_cases.name: delivery payload includes correct structure
preconditions: fixture(:webhook_event, %{webhook_id: webhook.id, tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :pending, event_type: 'story.status_changed', payload: %{story_id: story_id, old_status: 'pending', new_status: 'assigned'}})
HTTP mock captures the request body

technical_notes: Create Loopctl.Workers.WebhookDeliveryWorker as an Oban worker module. Queue: 'webhooks', max_attempts: 1 (we handle retry logic manually via snooze).
Use Req for HTTP delivery. Configure a 10-second receive_timeout.
Oban.insert/1 or Oban.insert_all/1 in the same Ecto.Multi as the event creation (Oban supports transactional job insertion).
Backoff schedule as a lookup list: [60, 300, 1500, 7200, 36000]. Index by (attempts - 1).
Worker flow: load webhook_event -> load webhook -> check active -> make HTTP POST -> update event status -> update webhook consecutive_failures.
Use Req.Test or Mox to mock HTTP requests in tests. Never make real HTTP calls in test.
Oban queue configuration in config: queues: [webhooks: 10].
Consider using Oban.Pro's SmartEngine if available, otherwise standard Oban retry with manual snooze scheduling.
